message:
fix(btree): four bw-tree audit defects (drop, consolidate, walk limit)

Multi-pass audit of crates/ffs-btree/src/bw_tree.rs surfaced four real
defects, including one stack-overflow regression that our existing
test suite never reached:

1. (Pass 3 — runtime risk) PageDelta forms a singly-linked list via
   next: Arc<Self>, and the default recursive Drop walks the chain
   through nested stack frames. Pre-consolidation keeps live chains
   short, but defer_reclaim hands the *old* chain (up to
   MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH = 16 384 nodes) to crossbeam-epoch for deferred
   drop right after a consolidation CAS. That drop runs on a worker
   thread with the default 2 MiB stack, where 16 K-deep recursion
   stack-overflows. Replaced with an iterative Drop that takes the
   `next` pointer, replaces it with empty_base, and walks the chain
   in a loop; only uniquely-owned Arcs are unwrapped, shared ones
   bail out so other owners can drive their own (necessarily shorter)
   drop chain.

2. (Pass 1 — public-fn audit) consolidate_page returned
   entries_count = 0 on its "already consolidated" early-return path,
   making the metric useless on healthy pages. Now reads the actual
   entry count from the Base node before returning.

3. (Pass 1 — public-fn audit) consolidate_all aborted on the first
   per-page consolidate_page failure, dropping the count of pages it
   had already finished. Per the docstring's "best effort, return
   how many succeeded" contract, per-page failures are now logged at
   warn level and skipped; the function still returns the count of
   successes.

4. (Pass 2 — error-path audit) chain_length and materialize_from_head
   used `> MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH` as their walk cap. Since pre-consolidation
   triggers at exactly that depth, multiple racing appenders can leave
   the chain at MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH + k for a brief window, and a
   concurrent materialize would then return Corruption for what is
   actually a healthy chain. Introduced MAX_CHAIN_WALK = 2 ×
   MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH as a runaway-loop guard distinct from the
   consolidation trigger.

Three regression tests added (entries_count on already-base page,
consolidate_all returning success count, materialize tolerating an
overshoot of MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH + 1 nodes) — the last test was the one
that exposed defect #1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
